headline: Net ROAS jumped from 59% to 96% (+37 pts) on 44% less spend — net loss narrowed from −$1.38M to −$75K, nearly reaching breakeven. AOV up 44% ($204 → $294) while orders fell 43%: a deliberate trade of volume for efficiency.
physical_takeaways: RS1 is the new anchor — didn't exist in June 2025. It's now the largest physical funnel by revenue.
357 held volume relatively well: 6,449 sales in June 2025, current data pending. AOV up vs. prior year ($245 → est. $360).
SF1 is offline — 3,064 FE sales and $889K in June 2025, zero this June. RS1 and SF2 absorbed that load.
AOV story is real: prior year blended AOV was $237. Current year estimated at $378 — a 60% jump driven by RS1 and mix shift away from SQSE/SQP.
SQSE/SQP/TOW/THR accounted for 3,460 sales in June 2025. Effectively deprecated this year — that volume has not been replaced 1:1.
digital_takeaways: SSTS collapsed: 3,032 sales in June 2025 to 285 in June 2026 (-91%). Single biggest volume loss in digital.
WPSS ($110K, $198 AOV) is new this year — didn't exist in June 2025. Partially offsets SSTS volume decline with higher AOV.
OSSF held steady: 885 to 877 FE sales (flat), AOV rose from $96 to $112. OP CVR dropped from 73% to 62%.
SRSW scaled from 53 to 339 FE sales (+540%), revenue from $4K to $31K. Small base but strong growth trajectory.
PSS June 2026 data pending — June 2025 PSS was 858 sales / $116K. Need to pull 2026 numbers to complete the rollup.

tests_in_progress.name: Putter CLP to RS1 PDP — Shopify

themes.title: Efficiency over volume — the business chose margin
themes.narrative: Spend dropped 46% and orders dropped 43%, but Net ROAS jumped from 44% to 111% and refund rate collapsed from 32% to 7%. The shift away from high-volume, high-refund digital funnels (led by SSG2 being retired) drove the biggest efficiency gain in recent memory.
themes.takeaway: Protect this efficiency — don't let spend creep back into low-quality volume.

themes.title: AOV is the growth lever that's working
themes.narrative: Physical AOV: +46% ($259 → $378). Digital AOV: +82% ($258 → $470). Higher-ticket funnels (RS1 at $400, PSS at $1,900, HTKT at $8,192) are replacing the commodity volume. The business is moving upmarket.
themes.takeaway: Double down on high-AOV funnels. Any new test should have an AOV hypothesis.

themes.title: PG1 v3 on Shopify is the structural bet
themes.narrative: Moving PG1 to Shopify and introducing a 7-day no-subscription free trial removes the forced continuity model that created refund exposure. Removing misleading checkout text (implying subscription when it was a free trial) addresses a trust and compliance issue at the highest-volume point in the funnel.
themes.takeaway: Free trial clarity at checkout is a CRO win and a retention investment — measure trial-to-paid conversion as the north star.

triumphs: Launched PG1 v3 on Shopify with a 7-day no-sub free trial — moving off forced continuity toward an opt-in model.
Removed misleading checkout text that implied customers were getting a subscription when they were actually starting a free trial.
11 tests concluded in June — 2 wins, 6 losses, 3 flat. 0546 (357 CoC) and 0545 (OSSF CoC) both shipped.
challenges: Free trial tracking is unreliable — the definition has changed multiple times, making it impossible to get a clean total count or a trustworthy trial-to-paid conversion rate. Need a single agreed-upon definition locked in before we can measure the PG1 v3 launch properly.

Trial-to-paid conversion rate is not yet measurable by platform — CoC and Shopify handle trials differently, and we can't compare them apples-to-apples until reporting is separated and standardized per platform.
